overall_assessment.fls_score_context: Your 337 FLS score represents a significant drop from your recent 500+ performances, primarily due to the 260-second completion time pushing you into penalty territory.
overall_assessment.session_headline: Solid technical execution with proper surgeon's knot and hand switching, but efficiency needs attention to return to your proven sub-90 second capability.

rubric_insights.observation: 260-second completion is well beyond your demonstrated 42-90 second range, suggesting either hesitation or technical difficulty during this attempt
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: Time penalty likely cost 200+ points compared to your typical performance

rubric_insights.observation: Excellent knot technique maintained - proper surgeon's knot with double wrap and consistent hand switching preserved from your successful attempts
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: No points lost here - this remains a strength

rubric_insights.observation: 1.5mm deviation from both marks is reasonable precision, costing only 3 penalty points
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: Minimal impact - well within acceptable range

technique_coaching.observation: The 65-second suture placement phase (frames 4-5) is significantly longer than your efficient attempts, suggesting extra movements or repositioning
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: This phase should be 15-25 seconds based on your history - excess time here cascades through the entire task
technique_coaching.correction: Focus on decisive needle entry and exit - minimize repositioning once you've identified your target points
technique_coaching.drill: Practice 20 rapid needle passes through marked targets, aiming for <20 seconds total placement time

technique_coaching.observation: Your throw phases (55s, 50s, 35s) are much longer than your proven 10-15 second capability per throw
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: You've demonstrated much faster knot tying - this suggests hesitation or lost muscle memory rather than technique failure
technique_coaching.correction: Return to your rapid throw rhythm - don't overthink the mechanics you've already mastered
technique_coaching.drill: Practice 10 consecutive surgeon's knots with 3-throw sequence, targeting 45 seconds total for all three throws

technique_coaching.observation: 25-second needle loading phase is acceptable but longer than your fastest attempts
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: Starting efficiently sets the rhythm for the entire task
technique_coaching.correction: Load needle decisively at 2/3 point from tip without hesitation
technique_coaching.drill: Practice 15 rapid needle loads, targeting <10 seconds each

strengths_to_reinforce.observation: Perfect surgeon's knot technique with clear double wrap formation maintained throughout
strengths_to_reinforce.why_it_matters: This is advanced technique that many trainees struggle with - you've mastered it completely
strengths_to_reinforce.encouragement: Your knot quality is excellent and consistent - this technical foundation is solid

strengths_to_reinforce.observation: Proper hand switching between all throws demonstrates good spatial awareness and technique retention
strengths_to_reinforce.why_it_matters: Hand switching prevents knot slippage and shows you understand proper surgical technique
strengths_to_reinforce.encouragement: Your hand switching is textbook perfect - this skill will serve you well in advanced procedures

strengths_to_reinforce.observation: Clean task completion with good drain closure and no technical failures
strengths_to_reinforce.why_it_matters: Task completion without errors shows your fundamental technique is sound
strengths_to_reinforce.encouragement: You completed everything correctly - this is about speed optimization, not learning new skills

practice_plan.session_focus: Restore your proven speed through rhythm and confidence - you have all the technical skills
practice_plan.warmup_drill: 5 minutes of rapid needle loading and simple knot tying to rebuild muscle memory

primary_drill.name: Speed Recovery Sequence
primary_drill.description: Complete full FLS Task 5 attempts focusing only on speed, not perfection. Set timer for 90 seconds and stop if not finished - this forces decisive movement. Repeat until you achieve sub-90 second completion.
primary_drill.success_criteria: At least one completion under 90 seconds with proper knot sequence

practice_plan.cooldown: Reflect on which movements felt rushed vs. which felt efficient - identify your optimal rhythm
practice_plan.estimated_sessions_to_improvement: 2-3 focused sessions to return to your 500+ FLS range

progress_context.vs_baseline: Significantly slower than your demonstrated 42-90 second capability
progress_context.vs_peak: This 260s performance is far below your consistent 500+ FLS scores
progress_context.milestone_note: Return to sub-90 second completion with maintained knot quality - you've done this before

coaching_limitations: Cannot assess fine needle angle details or tissue handling pressure from available camera angle. Unable to determine specific cause of timing regression without seeing hesitation points in detail.
